step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with all prior conclusions. Child mortality is defined universally by major health institutions including WHO and UNICEF as referring exclusively to children under five years of age. This reflects both epidemiological relevance—the highest risk of death occurs in infancy and toddlerhood—and policy consistency across national and international reporting systems.

Final answer: A

Agree. The inclusion of children under five captures the period of greatest vulnerability and aligns with standardized global definitions from WHO and UNICEF.
